title: Optical Coherence Elastography (OCE)
sub_domain: Sub-resolution displacement-tracked tissue mechanical property recovery (multi-physics joint inverse)
source_data_quality_note: v3 standalone multi-physics genesis Principle. Joint forward operator couples (i) optical coherence tomography (OCT) signal acquisition with (ii) tissue mechanical wave propagation under applied loading. Recovers spatially-resolved tissue mechanical properties (Young's modulus E(r), shear modulus mu(r), Lame parameters) at micrometer resolution by tracking sub-resolution displacements u(r, t) in OCT phase signal under static, dynamic, or wave-mode loading. Distinct from L1-042 OCT (intensity-based reflectivity recovery) and L1-047 elastography (US/MR-based at millimeter resolution) by primitive set, sensing_mechanism, and the displacement-tracking coupling. Authored under the standalone-as-default v3 design (see pwm-team/pwm_product/genesis/PWM_V3_STANDALONE_VS_COMPOSITE.md).
related_principles: L1-042 (OCT — coherence-based reflectivity acquisition component, informational)
L1-047 (Elastography — sibling tissue-stiffness imaging at coarser scale, d_principle ~ 0.45)
L1-207 (Linear elasticity — wave propagation primitive, informational)
L1-055 (MRE — sibling MR-based elastography, n_c ~ 0 in current genesis)

E.description: Optical Coherence Elastography (OCE): joint multi-physics forward couples (i) OCT signal acquisition where complex amplitude A(r) at depth z is recovered via low-coherence interferometry (spectral-domain or swept-source); (ii) sub-resolution displacement tracking where applied loading f_load(r, t) (compression, indentation, acoustic radiation force, or surface waves) produces displacement field u(r, t) that shifts the local scatter profile R(r - u(r, t)); (iii) tissue mechanical wave propagation governed by the linear elasticity wave equation rho * d2u/dt2 = grad . (sigma) + f_body, with constitutive law sigma = 2 mu epsilon + lambda tr(epsilon) I where epsilon is the symmetric strain tensor. The forward DAG has 8 primitives with two coupling constraints (n_c = 2): (i) Lagrangian-Eulerian displacement coupling — the deformation field u(r, t) shifts the OCT scatter profile, with phase difference Delta-phi = 2*k*u/n_g coupling phase-sensitive OCT signal to displacement; (ii) loading-to-displacement coupling — applied f_load produces u via the elasticity wave equation, with shear-wave speed c_s = sqrt(mu/rho) carrying shear-modulus information through the wave equation. Recovery is posed as the joint inverse problem that recovers (mu, lambda)(r) (or equivalently Young's modulus E(r) and Poisson ratio nu(r)) from time-resolved OCT phase data {Delta-phi(r, t)} given known applied loading f_load(r, t). Difficulty tier delta = 5 with raw condition number kappa ~ 250 (limited by OCT phase noise floor and wavelength-dependent decorrelation) and effective kappa_eff ~ 35 after physics-informed wave-equation regularization. Mismatch parameters: oct_phase_noise, scatter_decorrelation, loading_calibration_error, tissue_anisotropy, density_uncertainty, boundary_condition_uncertainty. Additive Gaussian phase noise sets the data-fidelity floor. See forward_model field for the closed-form joint imaging equation.
E.forward_model: OCT signal: A(r, t) = sqrt(R(r - u(r, t))) * exp(i * 2*k*z + i*phi_0); phase difference Delta-phi(r, t1, t2) = 2*k*[u_z(r, t2) - u_z(r, t1)] / n_g; elasticity wave: rho * d2u/dt2 = grad . sigma + f_body; sigma = 2 mu epsilon + lambda tr(epsilon) I; jointly invert {Delta-phi(r, t)} -> (mu, lambda)(r) given f_load(r, t), rho, boundary conditions
E.world_state_x: Spatially-resolved tissue mechanical properties: Lame parameters (mu, lambda)(r) [Pa] or equivalently Young's modulus E(r) and Poisson ratio nu(r). Range: corneal mu ~ 50-500 kPa; cancerous tissue 20-100 kPa stiffer than healthy stroma; vocal-fold superficial layer ~ 1-10 kPa; arterial wall ~ 50-1000 kPa.
E.observation_y: Time-resolved OCT phase difference {Delta-phi(r, t1, t2)} or full complex OCT signal {A(r, t)} acquired during applied loading sequence f_load(r, t).

G.dag: L.oct_acquisition -> L.phase_extraction -> L.displacement_tracking -> L.elasticity_wave -> L.constitutive_law -> L.applied_loading -> int.spatial -> int.temporal
coupling_constraints: lagrangian_to_eulerian: displacement field u(r, t) shifts OCT scatter profile R(r - u(r, t)); phase coupling Delta-phi = 2*k*u_z/n_g connects OCT phase signal to mechanical displacement (Doppler-like)

loading_to_displacement: applied f_load(r, t) produces u(r, t) via elasticity wave equation with shear-wave speed c_s = sqrt(mu/rho) carrying shear-modulus information; this is the coupling that makes OCE a multi-physics inverse rather than a pure OCT or pure mechanics problem

W.regime: Existence of recovered Lame parameter maps (mu, lambda)(r) is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds for shear-wave-mode loading at multiple frequencies (2D-3D shear-wave dispersion analysis); compression-mode and surface-wave OCE are conditionally unique requiring boundary-condition specification and density assumption. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~ 35 after physics-informed wave-equation regularization) — oct_phase_noise dominates displacement-tracking precision; tissue_anisotropy contributes off-diagonal Lame-tensor bias; density_uncertainty contributes a scaling factor of order rho^(-1/2). Joint Hadamard well-posedness for the coupled OCT-elasticity forward is established by Schmitt 1998 (foundational), Wang-Kirkpatrick-Hinds 2007 (phase-sensitive OCE), Kennedy-Wijesinghe-Sampson 2014 (compression OCE), Larin-Sampson 2017 (review), Kirby et al. 2017 (shear-wave OCE methods), and Wijesinghe et al. 2019 (computational OCE).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [physics-informed displacement-to-stiffness inversion; Helmholtz-equation regularized shear-wave inversion; finite-element-method forward + iterative gradient descent] | nonlinear-least-squares [Levenberg-Marquardt for parametric stiffness models] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [OCE-Net, ElastoNet]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||(mu, lambda)_hat - (mu, lambda)||_2 <= C1 * sigma_phase_noise * lambda_optical / (4*pi*n_g) / sqrt(N_frames). Wave-equation-induced bias adds + C2 * |loading_calibration_error| + C3 * |tissue_anisotropy| + C4 * |density_uncertainty| + C5 * ||(mu, lambda) - (mu, lambda)_s||_1 (best s-sparse / piecewise-smooth approximation residual). Helmholtz-regularized shear-wave inversion achieves q=2 (O(1/k^2)) under L2 prior; FEM-iterative methods achieve q=1 to q=2 depending on initialization. Deep-prior variants achieve faster initial convergence with bounded systematic bias. epsilon_fn captures monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * Z * N_frames * (axial_resolution_um)^(-1)) per iteration via FFT-based phase extraction + FEM-based wave-equation forward; learned variants O(H W Z N_frames * F_theta_cost) per forward pass

joint_well_posedness_references: Schmitt JM (1998) OCT elastography: imaging microscopic deformation and strain of tissue. Opt Express 3(6):199-211.
Wang RK, Kirkpatrick S, Hinds M (2007) Phase-sensitive optical coherence elastography for mapping tissue microstrains in real time. Appl Phys Lett 90(16):164105.
Kennedy BF, Wijesinghe P, Sampson DD (2014) The emergence of optical elastography in biomedicine. Nat Photon 8:743-755.
Larin KV, Sampson DD (2017) Optical coherence elastography: OCT at work in tissue biomechanics. Biomed Opt Express 8(2):1172-1202.
Kirby MA, Pelivanov I, Song S, Ambrozinski L, Yoon SJ, Gao L, Li D, Shen TT, Wang RK, O'Donnell M (2017) Optical coherence elastography in ophthalmology. J Biomed Opt 22(12):121720.

Mulligan JA, Untracht GR, Chandrasekaran SN, Brown CN, Adie SG (2016) Emerging approaches for high-resolution imaging of tissue biomechanics with optical coherence elastography. IEEE J Sel Top Quantum Electron 22(3):246-265.
Wijesinghe P, Sampson DD, Kennedy BF (2019) Computational optical palpation: a finite-element approach to micro-scale tactile imaging using a compliant sensor. J R Soc Interface 16(159):20190187.
Zvietcovich F, Larin KV (2022) Wave-based optical coherence elastography: the 10-year perspective. Prog Biomed Eng 4(1):012007.

distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-042 OCT recovers reflectivity / scatter-amplitude profile R(r) from coherence-domain detection; sensing_mechanism = oct_spectral_domain; n_c = 0; solution_space = 3D tissue reflectivity. L1-047 elastography (general) recovers stiffness at coarser scale via US or MR; sensing_mechanism = ultrasound_or_mri_based; coarser spatial resolution. L1-508 OCE recovers Lame-parameter maps (mu, lambda)(r) at micrometer resolution via the joint OCT + tissue-mechanics coupling — sensing_mechanism = phase_sensitive_oct_with_elasticity_wave; n_c = 2 (Lagrangian-Eulerian coupling + loading-to-displacement); adds L.elasticity_wave, L.constitutive_law, L.applied_loading primitives. Solution_space is 3D_lame_parameter_map, distinct from both L1-042's 3D reflectivity and L1-047's coarse-scale stiffness map. Jaccard distance over (sensing_mechanism, solution_space, primitives) ~ 0.45 from each parent — solidly Distinct under v1 advisory thresholds (>= 0.30).

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Emerging clinical and translational research: ophthalmology (corneal biomechanics — keratoconus screening, LASIK candidate evaluation, glaucoma), dermatology (skin cancer staging, lesion margin assessment), oncology (small-tumor stiffness mapping, breast lesion characterization), interventional cardiology (atherosclerotic plaque vulnerability assessment via intravascular OCE). Spatial resolution 10-50 micrometers — much finer than US elastography (~1 mm) or MRE (~3 mm). Active commercial development: Heidelberg, Optopol, Carl Zeiss Meditec working on integrated OCE add-ons to existing OCT platforms.
